title: Sequence clustering confounds AlphaFold2
description:
Main Topic: A critical evaluation of AF-cluster, a method used to predict the structures of metamorphic proteins with AlphaFold2 (AF2).

Key Points:

  • Metamorphic proteins can adopt multiple stable conformations, making their structure prediction challenging.
  • AF-cluster, initially presented as a solution, is called into question by Schafer et al. due to:
    • Inaccurate predictions compared to random sampling.
    • Misidentification of non-metamorphic proteins and erroneous conformation predictions.
    • Discrepancies in confidence scores for predicted structures.
  • CF-random, a method using ColabFold with random MSAs, is introduced as a more reliable and efficient alternative.
  • The study highlights the need for:
    • Rigorous validation of computational predictions.
    • Complementary approaches for accurate protein structure prediction.
    • Transparency in methodology to facilitate further research.
